free_text: Investigation of the photoeffect in light nuclei.
The cloud chamber had a diameter of 30 cm and a
height of 8 cm and was operated in a 10.5 x 10**3 Oe
In experiments with nitrogen and oxygen the cloud
chamber was filled to 326 and 265 mm Hg (at 15 deg C)
In the neon runs the chamber was filled with a 50%
neon and 50% hydrogen mixture to ~ 1000 mm Hg.
During the experiments the relative values of the
stopping power of nitrogen, oxygen, and neon after
At such stopping powers the track length of the
reactions were of the order of a few millimeters and
Data were compiled at the Russia MSU SINP
